claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children under 16 years old, or do you have any children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have children. I have three kids actually - Alex is 7, Blake is 12, and Charlie is 10. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! That's helpful to know you have three children all under 16.

Now, to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to understand your living situation:

**Do you live with all three of your children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, that's the thing - I don't actually live in the UK. I'm abroad at the moment. But Alex and Charlie do live with me here. Blake is a bit different though - he's been in care for a few months now, so he's not living with me day-to-day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's important information. Let me ask a follow-up question to better understand your situation:

**Which country are you currently living in with Alex and Charlie?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's an important detail because Child Benefit eligibility is normally tied to living in the UK.

Before I continue, I need to ask you a few more questions to fully understand your circumstances:

**Do you have British citizenship, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now, regarding Blake who is in care - I need to understand a bit more about his situation:

**When Blake went into care a few months ago, has he spent any time at home with you since then, and if so, roughly how many nights per week or days per week would you say he's been at home?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't been home at all since he went into care. He's been at the care home full-time - he hasn't come back to stay with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. That's important to know.

Now I need to ask about your situation living abroad in Spain:

**Are you a Crown servant (someone employed by the UK government), or do you have any other formal arrangement that would allow you to live abroad while maintaining your right to Child Benefit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I don't have any special government employment or formal arrangement - I'm just living here in Spain.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to clarify one more important thing about your living situation:

**While you're living in Spain with Alex and Charlie, are you still considered to be resident in the UK for tax purposes, or have you formally moved your residence to Spain?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I've moved to Spain - I'm not a UK resident for tax purposes anymore. I live here now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have one final question to help me fully assess your situation:

**For Alex and Charlie who are with you in Spain - are you paying for their accommodation, food, clothes, and other upkeep expenses out of your own money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I'm paying for their accommodation, food, clothes, and everything else they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all of that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review your situation:

- You have three children: Alex (7), Blake (12), and Charlie (10)
- You are a British citizen
- You are currently living in Spain and are no longer a UK resident for tax purposes
- Alex and Charlie live with you in Spain, and you pay for all their upkeep
- Blake has been in full-time care for a few months and hasn't spent any time at home with you

Based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules, I need to make an assessment for each child:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that you are not eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children based on your current circumstances.

**Summary:**

The main issue is that you are living in Spain and are no longer a UK resident for tax purposes. Child Benefit is designed for those who normally live in the UK. While there are some exceptions for people living abroad (such as Crown servants), Spain is not covered by these exceptions, and you don't have Crown servant status.

For **Alex and Charlie** specifically, even though you are responsible for them and paying for their upkeep, the fact that you're residing outside the UK means you cannot claim Child Benefit for them.

For **Blake**, there's an additional complication. Even if you were UK-resident, after being in care for a few months without spending any time at home, he would no longer qualify for Child Benefit (the standard rule allows 8 weeks in care before payments typically stop, unless the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home).

If your circumstances change—for example, if you were to move back to the UK—you could potentially reapply for Child Benefit at that time.

reasonings: Alex is not eligible for Child Benefit because you are not living in the UK. While you are a British citizen and responsible for Alex (paying for accommodation, food, and clothing), Child Benefit is only available to those living in the UK, except for Crown servants and those living in certain specific countries under special arrangements. Spain is not one of those countries covered under the Child Benefit abroad rules, and you are not a Crown servant.

Blake is not eligible for Child Benefit for two reasons: (1) You are not living in the UK, which is required for Child Benefit eligibility; and (2) Blake has been in care for a few months and has not spent any time at home with you. After 8 weeks in care, if a child does not spend at least 24 hours a week at home, Child Benefit entitlement normally ends. Since Blake has been in full-time care with no home visits, you would not meet the criteria for continued payment.

Charlie is not eligible for Child Benefit because you are not living in the UK. While you are a British citizen and responsible for Charlie (paying for accommodation, food, and clothing), Child Benefit is only available to those living in the UK, except for Crown servants and those living in certain specific countries under special arrangements. Spain is not one of those countries covered under the Child Benefit abroad rules, and you are not a Crown servant.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children. The primary reason is that you have moved to Spain and are no longer a UK resident for tax purposes. Child Benefit eligibility requires you to normally live in the UK, with limited exceptions for Crown servants and certain other countries. Spain does not fall within these exceptions, and you do not have a Crown servant status. Additionally, regarding Blake, even if you were UK-resident, his full-time placement in care without any home visits would make him ineligible after the 8-week period, as he does not meet the exception criteria of spending at least 24 hours per week at home.

Spain is not one of those countries covered under the Child Benefit abroad rules, and you are not a Crown servant.

response.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for any of your three children. The primary reason is that you have moved to Spain and are no longer a UK resident for tax purposes. Child Benefit eligibility requires you to normally live in the UK, with limited exceptions for Crown servants and certain other countries. Spain does not fall within these exceptions, and you do not have a Crown servant status. Additionally, regarding Blake, even if you were UK-resident, his full-time placement in care without any home visits would make him ineligible after the 8-week period, as he does not meet the exception criteria of spending at least 24 hours per week at home.
